subject: Anthropology
title: Defining Medical Anthropology
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Defining Medical Anthropology.

hook.text: A doctor sees a virus. A patient sees a curse. Medical anthropology studies why the same illness means different things.

buildup.text: Medical anthropology examines how cultures understand health, illness, healing, and the body differently.

discovery.text: It bridges biology and culture, showing that disease is biological but illness is always shaped by social context.

twist.text: Biomedicine itself is a cultural system. It has beliefs, rituals, and hierarchies — just like any healing tradition.

climax.text: Medical anthropology reveals that health is never purely biological. Culture shapes what we feel, fear, and try to heal.

punchline.text: Sickness is biological; illness is cultural.

quiz.question: What does medical anthropology study?
options: How cultures understand health, illness, and healing
